gloss.en: rules about right and wrong behavior

words.example: Medical ethics protect every patient.
She traveled across Europe last summer.

gloss.en: relating to Europe or its people

words.example: He studied European history at school.

gloss.en: judged the value or quality of something

words.example: The teacher evaluated each student's work.

gloss.en: judging the value or quality of something

words.example: We are evaluating two new plans.
Mr. Evans teaches our class.
Many events take place each year.
The doctor ran several examinations today.
The nurse examined his sore arm.
She is examining the old map.

gloss.en: things shown to explain a point

words.example: He gave two clear examples.
He was fined for exceeding the speed limit.

gloss.en: cases that do not follow the rule

words.example: There are a few exceptions to this rule.
His name was excluded from the list.
The price is ten dollars, excluding tax.

gloss.en: carried out or performed a plan

words.example: The team executed the plan well.

gloss.en: free from a duty or rule

words.example: Students are exempt from this fee.

gloss.en: freedom from a duty or rule

words.example: He claimed a tax exemption this year.

gloss.en: used a power or did physical activity

words.example: She exercised every morning before work.

gloss.en: tasks done for practice or fitness

words.example: We do these exercises every day.

gloss.en: items shown in a museum or show

words.example: The museum has many new exhibits.
The town existed long before us.
A simple answer exists for this.
The company expanded into new markets.
The city is expanding very fast.
The results met our expectations.

gloss.en: amounts of money that are spent

words.example: They cut their monthly expenditures.
Travel expenses were quite high.

gloss.en: things you have done or felt

words.example: She shared her travel experiences.
The students did simple experiments.

gloss.en: people with great skill or knowledge

words.example: The experts agreed on the cause.
He explained the rules to us.
This book explains the idea well.
The country's exports grew this year.
The workers were exposed to dust.
She expressed her thanks warmly.

gloss.en: looks or words that show feeling

words.example: Their faces had happy expressions.
They extended the deadline by a week.
We are extending the road north.
The path extends to the river.

gloss.en: the act of taking something out

words.example: The dentist did a quick extraction.
She closed her eyes and slept.
They faced many hard problems.
All the faces looked happy.
We are facing a tough choice.
The town has two big factories.
Many factors caused the change.
He checked the facts carefully.
He is failing his math class.
The old engine often fails.
Leaves are falling from the trees.
Snow falls every winter here.
Many families live on this street.

gloss.en: people who grow crops or raise animals

words.example: The farmers planted rice in spring.

gloss.en: areas of land for growing food

words.example: They visited two large farms.
Please run a little faster.
The fathers watched the game.

gloss.en: a feeling of being very tired

words.example: She felt deep fatigue after work.
This phone has new features.

gloss.en: a group of states or clubs joined together

words.example: The sports federation set new rules.

gloss.en: giving food to someone or something

words.example: She is feeding the small birds.
He hid his true feelings.
She feels happy today.
The school fees rose this year.

gloss.en: the parts of the body you stand on

words.example: My feet hurt after the walk.
He fell off the bike.
She felt very tired last night.

gloss.en: people or animals of the female sex

words.example: Most of the females stayed home.
This year we have fewer guests.
The cloth is made of natural fibers.

gloss.en: open areas of land or study

words.example: The fields were full of corn.
She picked ripe figs from the tree.
The sales figures look strong.

gloss.en: sent in or stored papers officially

words.example: She filed the report on time.

gloss.en: sets of papers or computer data

words.example: He saved all the files.
She is filing the new forms.
He filled the cup with water.
We watched two films tonight.
The project needs more financing.
The team shared their findings.
She always finds good books.

gloss.en: the parts at the end of the hand

words.example: He counted on his fingers.
They finished the work early.
We have a finite amount of time.

gloss.en: shot a weapon or ended a job

words.example: The boss fired two workers.
Several firms joined the deal.

gloss.en: places where fish are caught or raised

words.example: The fisheries support local families.

gloss.en: was the right size, or installed

words.example: The new coat fitted him well.

gloss.en: right or suitable for the situation

words.example: It was a fitting end to the day.

gloss.en: a state in the southern United States

words.example: They moved to Florida last year.
She picked some red flowers.
The river flows to the sea.
The market is in constant flux.
She focused on her studies.
The dog followed him home.
Night follows day.

The storm forced them inside.

gloss.en: powers that cause change or movement

words.example: Strong forces shaped the land.

gloss.en: a shallow place to cross a river

words.example: They crossed the river at the ford.

The forests are home to many animals.

They formed a new team.
Clouds are forming in the sky.

gloss.en: the careful making of a plan or mix

words.example: The new formulation works better.
Soldiers guarded the old fort.

gloss.en: took part in a battle or struggle

words.example: They fought hard for freedom.
Strong foundations hold the house up.

gloss.en: started or set up an organization

words.example: She founded a small school.

gloss.en: a crack or break, often in bone

words.example: The doctor found a small fracture.

Francis joined us for lunch.

gloss.en: a given name, as in San Francisco

words.example: They flew into San Francisco.

gloss.en: a common surname or given name

words.example: Franklin wrote many famous letters.

gloss.en: a short form of the name Frederick

words.example: Fred fixed my old car.

gloss.en: goods carried by ship, train, or truck
